QJL is a KV-cache state sketch comparator, not a defeated native baseline.
notes: QJL uses a JL transform and sign-bit quantization for KV-cache compression.

overclaim_guard: KIVI compresses KV cache state and still requires native quality/throughput comparison.
notes: KIVI is a tuning-free asymmetric 2-bit KV-cache quantization method.

overclaim_guard: Q-KVComm transmits compressed KV-derived state; do not claim this is a source-private packet.
notes: Q-KVComm transmits compressed KV cache representations with reported 5-6x compression.

overclaim_guard: KVQuant is a KV-cache quantization comparator; do not claim it is a packet baseline.
notes: KVQuant targets accurate sub-4-bit KV-cache quantization.

overclaim_guard: TurboQuant is a vector/KV quantizer; byte floors alone do not compare native latency.
notes: TurboQuant reports quality-neutral KV-cache quantization at 3.5 bits per channel.

overclaim_guard: KVComm shares selected KV pairs; this is only a native-pending 30%-layer byte floor.
